Hindsight Phase A+B (Python client, BM25s). 3/30 exact, 5/30 LLM. Almost all max_turns — FINAL() not called.

Hindsight Rust (native tantivy). 8/30 exact. Q830+Q1030+Q853 exact.

C2: top-50 rerank pool. 11/30 exact, 14/30 fuzzy. Worse than C1 top-200 (16/30 fuzzy). Q1034+Q1115 new but lost 5.
C3: query condensation + 8192 tokens. 13/30 exact, 14/30 fuzzy. Q1029 FIRST EVER CORRECT!
C4: ColBERT first-stage retriever + passage. 9/30 exact, 11/30 fuzzy. Below C1 reranker (16/30).
C4 with RRF fusion (not augmentation). 9/30 exact, 12/30 fuzzy. +1 over augmentation but still below C1 reranker (16/30).
C4 RRF with nprobes=1+refine=2. 8/30 exact, 10/30 fuzzy. Slight regression from slow C4 (12/30) but much faster.
C7: passage-only → ColBERT rerank. 7/30 exact, 10/30 fuzzy. Worse than C1 (16/30). Passage-only has narrower coverage.
ColBERT primary retriever + ColBERT reranker. 9/30 exact, 12/30 fuzzy. Q1029 correct! Below C1 (16/30) — extraction bottleneck.
ColBERT retrieval + passage snippets + reranker. 7/30 exact, 8/30 fuzzy. Q706+Q893 FIRST EVER CORRECT!
4096-byte passages + ColBERT rerank. 11/30 exact, 14/30 fuzzy. Close to C1 (16/30), more efficient (95K tokens).
4096 passages + doc RRF + ColBERT rerank. 11/30 exact, 12/30 fuzzy. Doc RRF hurts again.
4096 passage-only (no doc RRF) + rerank. 7/30 exact. Doc RRF actually helps for 4096 passages.
H2H: 512 passages + doc RRF + rerank. 11/30 exact, 14/30 fuzzy.
H2H: 4096 passages + doc RRF + rerank. 13/30 exact, 15/30 fuzzy. Q706+Q747!
Hierarchical passages (512-4096) + title metadata + doc RRF + rerank. 10/30 exact, 11/30 fuzzy. Q893 correct! Most efficient (93K tokens).
ColBERT retrieval + hier passage snippets (title) + rerank. 9/30 exact, 12/30 fuzzy. Q706 correct.
ColBERT retrieval + passage expansion rerank. 10/30 exact, 12/30 fuzzy. Q747 correct. +4 over no expansion.
gw-kb hybrid search (BM25+vector+topic-membership RRF), search-only prompt. 6/30 exact, 6/30 fuzzy. Gold doc recall 21/30 but only 4/30 read. Apostrophe bug (fixed post-run).
gw-kb + kb_topic/kb_topics tools. 4/30 exact, 4/30 fuzzy. Topic browsing wastes turns — answer rate dropped from 12/30 to 11/30.

gw-kb + kb_topic/kb_topics/kb_explore tools. 2/30 exact, 2/30 fuzzy. Worst variant — agent enters explore loops (q853: 36 kb_topic + 17 kb_explore). Topic summaries contain answers but agent can't extract them.

R9 diagnostic: qwen3 backend at --k 50 (vs k=10). 4/29 exact = 4/29 fuzzy (1 query aborted by ouros heap panic). Same as k=10's 4/30 — agent can't RECOGNIZE gold even with 5x more candidates visible. Visibility is not the bottleneck; prompt-retriever mismatch is. GEPA co-optimization (R6) required. Also introduces bench/browsecomp/run_per_query.sh driver that isolates ouros crashes per-query.

Clean baseline on this branch: native tantivy BM25 + default SYSTEM_PROMPT + qwen3.5:9b. 6/29 exact, 7/29 fuzzy. Correct: 159, 191, 464, 689, 747, 797, 853. Anchors subsequent experiments; within variance of historical "1e40c6c-9b" (8/30).

GEPA-v3 prompt (configs/gepa_best_v3.txt) on same native BM25 stack. 6/29 exact, 7/29 fuzzy — ties baseline, burns 2x tokens. Correct: 175, 191, 464, 689, 763, 797, 853. Complementary to baseline (union=9/30, 2 unique each: baseline=159+747, gepa-v3=175+763), suggesting prompt ensembling could help, but no single-prompt win.

Passage+doc BM25 RRF (4096-byte chunks, 892K passages, no rerank). 11/29 exact, 13/29 fuzzy — +5/+6 over native BM25 baseline. Gained: 1036, 1128, 469, 572, 763, 830, 885. Lost only 689. Close to historical passages-rerank-c1 (13/30 exact, 16/30 fuzzy) without needing the ColBERT rerank server. Uses `search_with_passages` fusion path that was already wired in.

Hierarchical passage index (chunks 512+1024+2048+4096, 15M passages). 9/29 exact = 9/29 fuzzy — regression of -2/-4 from 4096-only. Smaller chunks have higher term density and outrank larger chunks; the 512-byte snippets are too narrow for reliable extraction. 4096-only remains the sweet spot.

Passage+doc RRF (4096, retrieve_k=200) → ColBERT blob rerank (rerank_server_blobs.py on 8001 with data/passage-blobs). 7/29 exact = 7/29 fuzzy — regression of -4/-6 from passage-only (13/29 fuzzy). Lost {1036,1128,747,763,830,853,885}, gained {152}. ColBERT's semantic similarity drops BM25's keyword-matched gold docs — matches prior finding that rerank hurts passage-level retrieval on entity-heavy queries. Historical passages-rerank-c1 hit 13/30 exact on same nominal stack, so some of the gap may be run-to-run variance or blob-store/passage-index mismatch, but not worth debugging when the no-rerank variant is already near the old best.

Passage+doc RRF at --k 15 (vs k=10). 6/29 exact, 7/29 fuzzy — regression to baseline-BM25 level. Lost 7 (1036, 469, 747, 763, 797, 830, 885), gained 1 (625). 9B attention can't handle 15 docs in context; k=10 is the hard ceiling on this stack.

Variance check (same config as apr19-passages-4096). 10/29 exact, 12/29 fuzzy. Run-to-run variance on the winner is ±1 on both metrics. BUT union across the two runs = 18/30 (intersect = 7), so ~11 queries "flip" between correct and incorrect across runs — room for a best-of-N/self-consistency ensemble IF we can avoid prior voting failures where wrong answers agreed more than rare correct ones.

Passage+RRF with context_snippet_chars 300→500. 7/29 exact, 9/29 fuzzy — regression of -3/-3 below variance band. Longer snippets (500×10=5000 chars vs 3000) overwhelm 9B attention in the same way k=15 did. The context budget at ~3000 chars is the real ceiling, not the per-snippet granularity.
